actions.resetProgressConfirm: This will clear all tutorial completion status. You'll see first-visit prompts again.

empty.noTutorials: No tutorials available for this page.
empty.noTutorialsDescription: Tutorials will appear here when you navigate to pages that have guided tours.

prompt.title: New to this page?
prompt.description: Take a quick tour to learn the key features.

basics.label: Chat Basics
basics.description: Learn how to select models and send your first message
basics.modelContent: Select an AI model to chat with. Different models have different capabilities - look for tags like 'Vision' or 'Fast'. Star your favorites for quick access.
basics.inputContent: Type your message here and press Enter to send. You can ask questions, request help with tasks, or have a conversation.
basics.slashContent: Type / to see available commands. Try /search to find content in your knowledge base, or /web to search the internet.
basics.promptContent: Select a system prompt to guide how the AI responds. You can create custom prompts in the Prompts workspace.
basics.newChatContent: Click here to start a new conversation. Your previous chats are saved in the sidebar for easy access.

tools.label: Tools & Attachments
tools.description: Discover knowledge search, file uploads, and advanced tools
tools.toolsButtonContent: Access knowledge search, upload files, configure MCP tools, and more advanced features here.
tools.knowledgeContent: Enable knowledge search to let the AI reference your ingested documents, notes, and media. Great for research and fact-checking.
tools.webSearchContent: Toggle web search to let the AI search the internet for current information. Perfect for recent news or topics not in your knowledge base.
tools.uploadContent: Upload images or documents to discuss them with the AI. Vision-capable models can analyze images directly.
tools.ingestContent: Use Quick Ingest to add web pages, PDFs, or other content to your knowledge base for later reference.
tools.mcpContent: MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools let the AI interact with external services and execute actions on your behalf.

voice.label: Voice Chat
voice.description: Learn to use voice input and have spoken conversations
voice.voiceChatContent: Start a hands-free voice conversation. Speak naturally and hear AI responses aloud.
voice.dictationContent: Use dictation to speak your messages instead of typing. Great for longer inputs or when your hands are busy.
voice.ttsContent: Click on any AI response to have it read aloud. Customize the voice and speed in Settings.
voice.settingsContent: Configure voice chat settings including the voice model, auto-send timing, and trigger phrases.

basics.label: Research Studio Basics
basics.description: Learn the three-pane workspace flow: sources, chat, and studio outputs
basics.headerContent: Use the header to rename, switch, import, and manage workspaces while tracking system status.
basics.sourcesContent: Add and select sources here. Your selected source set controls what the chat and studio use.
basics.chatContent: Ask questions against your selected sources and review grounded answers before generating outputs.
basics.studioContent: Turn source context into summaries, reports, quizzes, flashcards, and other artifacts.
basics.switcherContent: Open the workspace switcher to jump between saved workspaces and continue different projects.

basics.label: Media Basics
basics.description: Search, filter, inspect, and manage ingested media in the library
basics.searchContent: Start with a query here to find items by title or content.
basics.filtersContent: Refine by media types, metadata, keywords, and collection filters before running searches.
basics.submitContent: Submit your query after adjusting filters to refresh the result set.
basics.resultsContent: Review matched items, select one, and navigate pages to inspect more content.
basics.viewerContent: Inspect selected content, metadata, and navigation details in the main viewer pane.
basics.toolsContent: Open library tools for ingest job monitoring and storage/usage statistics.

basics.label: Knowledge Basics
basics.description: Search your indexed sources and review grounded answers with citations
basics.searchContent: Enter a focused question about your docs, notes, or media to start retrieval.
basics.sourcesContent: Select which source groups to query so answers stay scoped to what you care about.
basics.contextContent: Adjust retrieval preset and context options before running your search.
basics.historyContent: Reuse prior threads and queries from history so you can continue investigations quickly.
basics.resultsContent: Review answer output, cited sources, and evidence once a search completes.

basics.label: Characters Basics
basics.description: Create, filter, and manage reusable character assistants for chat workflows
basics.newContent: Start here to create a new character with persona, prompt, greeting, and metadata.
basics.searchContent: Filter your character library by name, description, tags, or prompt content.
basics.scopeContent: Switch between active and deleted characters to recover or permanently remove entries.
basics.viewContent: Toggle between table and gallery layouts based on whether you need dense controls or visual browsing.
basics.libraryContent: Open character actions to chat, quick-chat, edit, duplicate, export, or review version history.

basics.label: Prompts Basics
basics.description: Create reusable prompts, search your library, and manage prompt workflow segments
basics.segmentedContent: Switch between Custom, Copilot, Studio, and Trash to manage different prompt sources.
basics.createContent: Create a reusable prompt template you can insert into chat conversations.
basics.searchContent: Search prompt names, content, and keywords to quickly find the right template.
basics.filtersContent: Use type and tag filters to narrow your prompt list before editing or running prompts.
basics.transferContent: Export prompts for backup or sharing, and import prompt sets to bootstrap new workflows.

basics.label: Evaluations Basics
basics.description: Create evaluations, inspect recent runs, and navigate evaluation tabs
basics.headerContent: Use this workspace to define evaluation specs and monitor quality over time.
basics.tabsContent: Switch between Evaluations, Runs, Datasets, Webhooks, and History depending on your workflow stage.
basics.createContent: Start here to define a new evaluation and attach a dataset or inline samples.
basics.listContent: Select an evaluation from this list to inspect details, edit it, or run it.
basics.detailContent: Review selected evaluation metadata and copy IDs needed for automation and debugging.

basics.label: Notes Basics
basics.description: Organize notes, apply filters, and edit content with split list/editor workflow
basics.listContent: Browse and select notes from the sidebar to load them in the editor.
basics.modeContent: Switch between active notes and trash to recover or permanently remove entries.
basics.filtersContent: Sort by date/title and use notebook/keyword filters to narrow your working set.
basics.editorContent: Edit note content, references, and metadata in the main editor panel.
basics.saveContent: Save updates, duplicate notes, export content, and run helper actions from the header.

basics.label: Flashcards Basics
basics.description: Use study, manage, and transfer flows to maintain spaced-repetition decks
basics.tabsContent: Switch tabs to study cards, manage deck contents, and import or export card data.
basics.reviewContent: Run review sessions here and score recall quality to update spaced-repetition schedules.
basics.manageContent: Open the Manage tab to search cards, filter by deck/tags, and perform bulk updates.
basics.transferContent: Use Transfer for CSV/JSON/APKG import and export workflows.
basics.quizContent: Jump into Quiz mode from your selected deck to validate retention with assessments.

basics.label: Content Controls Basics
basics.description: Learn how to set up content safety rules, blocklists, and test moderation.
basics.heroContent: This is your content safety hub. The status badge shows whether your server is connected.
basics.policyContent: Start here. Set your base content safety policy — what categories to filter and how strictly.
basics.blocklistContent: Add specific words, phrases, or patterns you want to always block or flag.
basics.testContent: Try your rules in real time. Type a message and see whether it would be allowed or blocked.

basics.label: World Books Basics
basics.description: Create world books, filter by state, and manage reusable lore entries
basics.headerContent: World Books hold reusable lore and structured context that can be attached to characters and chat sessions.
basics.searchContent: Search by title, metadata, and content to quickly locate the right world book.
basics.filtersContent: Filter by enabled/disabled state and attachment status before editing or exporting.
basics.tableContent: Select a row to manage entries, attachments, and advanced actions for each world book.
basics.actionsContent: Create a new world book or import an existing JSON bundle to seed your knowledge base.

basics.label: MCP Hub Basics
basics.description: Learn how to browse tools, connect servers, and manage permissions.
basics.welcomeContent: MCP Hub manages external AI tools. Start here to see what tools are available and connect new servers.
basics.catalogContent: Browse all registered tools here. Each tool shows its capabilities and risk level.
basics.credentialsContent: Connect external MCP servers here. Each server provides additional tools for your AI assistant.
basics.profilesContent: Create permission profiles to control which tools each user or persona can access.
basics.auditContent: Review policy findings and configuration issues across all your MCP Hub settings.

basics.label: Quiz Basics
basics.description: Learn how to generate, take, and review quizzes from your media content.
basics.tabsContent: The Quiz Playground has five tabs. Generate quizzes from your media, create them manually, take quizzes, manage your library, and review results.
basics.generateContent: Start here. Select a video, article, or document from your media library, and the AI will generate quiz questions for you.
basics.createContent: Prefer to write your own? Create quizzes with multiple choice, true/false, fill-in-the-blank, and matching questions.
basics.takeContent: Once you have quizzes, come here to take them. Your answers are saved and scored automatically.
basics.resultsContent: See your scores, review incorrect answers, and track your progress over time.

basics.label: Watchlists Basics
basics.description: Learn how to add feeds, create monitors, and review collected articles
basics.setupContent: Start here to add your first feed and create a monitor. The guided setup walks you through it step by step.
basics.feedsContent: Feeds are sources like RSS feeds or websites that your monitors check for new content.
basics.monitorsContent: Monitors run on a schedule to fetch and process content from your feeds. Choose a preset schedule like daily or hourly.
basics.articlesContent: Articles are collected content from successful monitor runs. Switch to the Articles tab to browse and read them.

basics.label: Monitoring Basics
basics.description: Learn how to monitor server health and set up alert rules
basics.overviewContent: This section shows your server's current health metrics and security status. Use the refresh button or enable auto-refresh to keep it up to date.
basics.createRuleContent: Set up rules to be notified when metrics cross thresholds. Pick a metric name, set a threshold and duration, then choose a severity level.
basics.historyContent: When alerts trigger, they appear here. You can assign alerts to yourself, snooze them, or escalate to critical.
